# Questions in Livestream 59

Live Stream #59: No Space Like Home

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:26</a> | What is the origin of dragon legends: fossils, memory, or something else? | He said fossils may explain some cases, but the wider pattern probably reaches back into very old mythic and anthropological memory and remains uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=681" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:21</a> | What similarities did Egyptian priests and Levitical priests share? | He emphasized ritual purity practices, especially bathing, clean clothing, and avoidance of certain animal products in sacred service. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:23</a> | What Old Testament or New Testament backgrounds resource book would he recommend? | He recommended bibliography-driven New Testament starting points and, for the Old Testament, `Behind the Scenes of the Old Testament` as a strong college-level resource. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=1027" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:07</a> | Does the absence of pig bones at Avaris really matter? | Yes. He treated it as meaningful because pig avoidance was an Egyptian taboo, not just a generic Semitic custom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=1217" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:17</a> | If most of the Torah goes back to the Late Bronze Age, are there still later additions? | Yes. He said the core is early, but some framing passages, glosses, and closing material are later than Moses and likely extend into the conquest, judges, or early monarchy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=1510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:10</a> | Who was Balaam son of Beor, and why was he killed? | He described Balaam as a prophet-for-hire who could not curse Israel directly, then helped lead Israel into sin and was judged for throwing his lot in with Israel's enemies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=1787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:47</a> | What Hebrew word would he suggest for someone who has just learned the alphabet? | He suggested `sus` or `susa`, the Hebrew words for horse and mare, because the forms help teach masculine, feminine, and plural patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=1885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:25</a> | Was the Roman Colosseum built from the remains of the Second Temple? | Indirectly, yes. He said the materials were not hauled from Jerusalem, but the Colosseum was funded from the Roman booty taken from the temple. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=2062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:22</a> | Are there signs that Ramesses II lost his army around 1265 BC, and do records from that period help? | He said the records are sparse, but Ramesses stopped campaigning after year 17, Egypt soon lost Levantine holdings, and the loss of chariot strength is a plausible explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=2350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:10</a> | How would the twelve disciples have understood Matthew 10:23? | He said the emphasis is on persecution and continued flight from city to city, while it is hard to know whether the disciples understood the coming of the Son of Man as resurrection, second coming, or something broader. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=2686" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:46</a> | Can Cassius Dio's account of phantoms during Nero's canal work be trusted, or is there another explanation? | He thought the supernatural details were likely rhetorical embellishment, but that workers digging through graves could have found bones and reacted with fear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=3076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:16</a> | In Daniel 8:9, does the small horn come out of one of the four horns or one of the four winds? | He leaned toward `one of the four winds`, arguing that the feminine grammatical antecedent and the nearer reference fit that reading better. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=3512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:32</a> | If he does an eschatology video, will it cover views like futurism and preterism? | Yes. He said he would want to present the major views, though his own current leaning is toward a non-dispensational futurism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=3664" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:04</a> | Is Genesis the foundation of the Christian faith, or is the Messiah? | He said the foundation is Jesus Christ, while the fall of humanity is an essential doctrine that does not require a specific young-earth reading of Genesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=3886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:46</a> | In Genesis 34:25, did only Simeon and Levi kill Shechem's males, or did they have an army with them? | He said Simeon and Levi probably led the action with household servants or clan retainers, and he framed it as clan justice after treachery by both sides. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=4360" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:40</a> | What is the best resource for understanding the age of the patriarchs if Jacob was not literally seventy when he married Rachel? | He suggested Richard Hess on ancient Israelite religion and resources on clans, arguing that the long ages may represent clan lifespans or omitted intermediaries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=4585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:25</a> | Could someone give a plausible naturalistic explanation for the archaeological and biblical data behind the Exodus and conquest? | He said many events can be described as natural or hyper-natural, but the timing is theologically significant because God is working through nature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=4929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:09</a> | Does Josephus linking Keturah's descendants with Heracles mean educated Jews believed in gentile mythological heroes? | He said educated Jews were immersed in Greco-Roman mythology, but Josephus was often unreliable when filling ancient gaps with inherited myth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=5160" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:00</a> | What does `herem` mean, especially the definition `devote to destruction`? | He said the core sense is devotion or separation, while destruction is an inferred contextual meaning and not the whole lexical range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=5505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:45</a> | What script and writing material would Moses have used? | He said Moses likely used an alphabetic script, a quill, carbon-based ink, and writing surfaces such as papyrus, parchment, or vellum available from Egypt and the wilderness flocks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=5772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:12</a> | Would he review an Afrocentric video about Egyptians being Black? | He said Afrocentrism is fair game for a popcorn review if the video is the right length, legally low-risk, educational, and not just repeated focus on the same channel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6014" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:14</a> | If the Hyksos were fellow Semites, why were they set apart from Joseph's brothers in Genesis 43:32? | He said the verse refers to native Egyptians, not Hyksos, and Avaris was a multiethnic city where Egyptians could still keep food taboos against Semites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6221" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:41</a> | What are good sources on pre-Israelite prophethood or shamanism? | He pointed again to Hess on ancient Israelite religion and noted that most evidence is archaeological, with the later Balaam inscription as one important extra-biblical text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6376" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:16</a> | Are there ancient Egyptian words, names, or idioms still in use in modern English? | He gave `Mary` as the clearest example, tracing it back through Hebrew to an Egyptian root meaning beloved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6505" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:25</a> | Were pork-hating people only special to Avaris? | No. He said Egyptians broadly disliked pig farming because pigs polluted waterways and canals, so this was not just an Avaris-specific pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6629" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:29</a> | Do the different Hebrew words for men in Leviticus 20:18, `ish` and `zakar`, affect interpretation? | He said they add nuance: `ish` suggests a sexually mature man, while `zakar` means a male of unspecified age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=6848" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:08</a> | Regarding Daniel 1:1, what are his thoughts on the Babylonian armies at Carchemish and Pharaoh Necho? | He described Carchemish as pivotal: Assyria was crushed, Necho withdrew through Canaan, and Nebuchadnezzar then secured Babylonian control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=7287" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:27</a> | Are there clues in Genesis that Joseph's story took place in the Hyksos period, and can we tell the rulers were not native Egyptians? | He pointed to chariot use and the later king who did not know Joseph as clues, but said the text itself is clearer about regime change than about the rulers' ethnicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=7891" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:31</a> | What does he think of Bowen's `Atheist Handbook to the Old Testament`? | He had not read it yet, but said some arguments he had encountered from Bowen seemed weak or misdirected, such as claims about Tyre. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=8071" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:31</a> | What does he think of Omm Sety? | He described her as a curiosity and popularizer connected with the Temple of Seti I, but not an important scholarly figure in Egyptology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=8263" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:43</a> | What mainly motivated Egyptian conquests and vassal states? | He said tribute was the main motive: Egypt wanted goods and metals to support its economy, not costly direct imperial administration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=8461" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:01</a> | Why were the Egyptians unable or unwilling to stop the Philistine takeover of the coast, and what effect did that have? | He said Egypt likely lacked enough chariot strength after the Reed Sea event, which let the Philistines take the coast and helped open the way for Israelite settlement inland. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=8834" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:14</a> | Who is the smaller horn of Daniel 8? | He identified it with the antichrist, but acknowledged he could not quickly find the supporting Revelation passage and wanted to revisit the issue in a fuller eschatology study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=9085" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:25</a> | Are there plans to speed publication of the remaining Oxyrhynchus papyri using machine learning? | He said publication has slowed because funding and interest have declined, and he was skeptical that machine learning alone could solve the decipherment work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=9489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:09</a> | Is Francesca Stavrakopoulou a respected scholar in Old Testament studies? | He said the answer depends on the audience: liberal and atheist circles may value her, but conservative and many moderate scholars regard her views as fringe or poorly supported. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=9632" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:32</a> | Why does the Exodus tradition not mention the temple of Baal at Avaris? | He thought the silence was deliberate: the text suppresses Baal's memory to stop Israel from carrying that cult identity into Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=9898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:58</a> | Has he considered writing archaeological fiction? | Yes, especially archaeological science fiction rather than adventure fiction, but he said time has kept him from finishing the project. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=10030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:10</a> | How much do we know about the Philistines from archaeology, and do we have texts from their point of view? | He said we know some archaeology from Philistine cities and iconography, but very little written material from their own perspective on biblical events. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=10259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:59</a> | Is mixing soft drinks at a restaurant fountain a sin? | He treated it humorously and said mixing sodas is more bad taste than a kosher violation, as long as it does not mix milk-like ingredients. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=10385" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:05</a> | Why do pyramids lack hieroglyphs inside while Valley of the Kings tombs have them, and are there hieroglyphs depicting pyramids? | He said some later pyramids do have texts, earlier royal writing was often in surrounding temples and tombs, and the surrounding necropolis securely identifies the pyramid owners. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=10933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:13</a> | What does he think of the chronology differences between the Septuagint and Masoretic Text, including 430 versus 215 years in Egypt? | He generally preferred the Septuagint numbers for Genesis 5 and 11 and the reading of 430 years in Canaan and Egypt, but cautioned that each number needs case-by-case text-critical work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/RqA8RW50z8I?t=11271" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:51</a> | Do we have texts or folklore about the Philistine god Dagon? | Very little. He said Dagon is best understood as a grain deity, not a fish god, and our evidence about him is sparse. |
